title: Which of the following is not included in the

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which of the following is not included in the 
exemptions for rooming-in and breastfeeding?
a. Infants whose mothers are seriously ill
b. Infants who have been delivered outside health 
institutions
c. Infants whose mothers are taking medications 
contraindicated to breast-feeding
d. Infants whose mothers are violent psychotics
e. Infants whose conditions do not permit roomingin and breast-feeding as determined by the 
attending physician
f. Infants whose mothers have conditions that do 
not permit breast-feeding and rooming-in as 
determined by the attending physician
g. None of the choices

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: g. None of the choices. All of the options are included in the exemptions for rooming-in and breastfeeding.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: show me the section of the law

turns.speaker: assistant
assistant
turns.text:
Option b, "Infants who have been delivered outside health institutions," is exempted from mandatory rooming-in and breastfeeding because these infants may not be immediately brought to a health facility where the mother can room-in and breastfeed. In such cases, the mother and infant may be separated for a period of time until they are able to access appropriate health services.

However, it is important to note that while immediate rooming-in and breastfeeding may not be possible for infants who have been delivered outside health institutions, efforts should still be made to promote and support breastfeeding as soon as possible, such as through the provision of breast milk substitutes or assistance with expressing milk. This is in line with the overall goal of the Expanded Breastfeeding Promotion Act of 2009 to promote and protect breastfeeding as a vital aspect of infant and young child health and development.
